channel.title: Routine on Artem Yegorov
channel.description: Recent content in Routine on Artem Yegorov

item.title: Ruby One-Liners
item.description: One of the coolest things about Ruby is that you can write clear yet concise code. This is especially useful when you want to automate repetitive tasks using scripts. To understand how to use Ruby in your scripts, in most cases you can simply look at the standard help for the ruby command, such as ruby -h or man ruby. You can also pass arbitrary code using the -e option, for example:
